meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding: Finish Your First SaaS Project
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement high-conversion user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate developer tutorial.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects isn't more code; it's momentum. By implementing a frictionless user onboarding flow, you validate your product's value proposition immediately. This keeps users engaged, reduces churn, and provides the psychological win you need to ship, deploy, and finally cross the finish line of your first SaaS.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions and unfinished features. You start with a vision, code for a week, and then the complexity wall hits. The excitement fades, the scope creeps, and the project becomes another 'in-progress' relic. This is the intermediate developer's trap: building features instead of building value.

But what if you stopped building for yourself and started building for the user's first thirty seconds? User onboarding isn't just a welcome screen; it's a strategic framework that forces you to define your core value proposition. By focusing on the 'Aha!' moment, you simplify your scope, accelerate development, and build the confidence required to ship production-ready applications.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your app's value. We need to strip away features to focus on this singular outcome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value of my SaaS project and propose a 3-step onboarding flow that drives the user to the primary 'Aha!' moment as quickly as possible.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current codebase and suggest the most critical user action to trigger on first login.'

steps.title: Architect the Onboarding State
steps.description: Create a clean state machine for the onboarding process so users don't get trapped in a loop.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a state machine schema for a 3-step onboarding flow. Include states: initial_welcome, tutorial_overlay, and feature_activation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a state machine utility in src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts to manage flow progress.'

steps.title: Implement Progressive Disclosure
steps.description: Hide complex settings behind the onboarding flow. Only show what is necessary to get started.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my dashboard component to hide advanced settings behind an 'onboardingComplete' flag, showing only the core feature set initially.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor Dashboard.tsx to implement conditional rendering based on onboarding status.'

steps.title: Build the Guided Tour
steps.description: Add non-intrusive markers that highlight the main CTA. Don't block the UI; guide it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a React component design for a lightweight, non-blocking onboarding tooltip that highlights the main CTA button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create Tooltip.tsx component and integrate it into the main action button.'

steps.title: Validate and Deploy
steps.description: Test the flow to ensure users reach the goal without friction. Use Claude Code to run automated tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a set of end-to-end test cases for the new user onboarding flow using Playwright.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run E2E tests for the onboarding flow and fix any accessibility issues found.'

pro_tips: Never ask for user data before providing value; prioritize the 'Aha!' moment over account registration.
Use 'Empty States' to teach, not just to inform; turn a blank dashboard into a call to action.
Keep your onboarding flow under 60 seconds; if it takes longer, your product is too complex for a v1.
Instrument your onboarding with event tracking from day one to see exactly where users drop off.
Treat onboarding as a living feature; iterate on it based on real user session recordings, not your assumptions.
content.next_steps: After completing your onboarding flow, deploy your application to a staging environment and share it with three peers to gather immediate feedback on the user journey.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a stagnant project into a polished, user-ready application. Stop letting your code gather digital dust. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
